title: Micranthemum erosum
intro: Micranthemum erosum, also known as creeping joynair or creeping kidneywort, is a low-growing, perennial herb native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and reduce fever. Bioactive constituents of Micranthemum erosum include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as phenolic acids and terpenes. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Micranthemum erosum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is native to South America and is commonly known as 'Dwarf Hedging Plantain' or 'Brazilian Dwarf Baby Tears'. This aquatic plant is also known as 'Brazilian Baby Tears' or 'Dwarf Baby Tears', and is widely used in aquariums and water gardens. The classification of Micranthemum erosum includes it as a member of the genus Micranthemum, which comprises several species of aquatic plants. Micranthemum erosum is often cultivated for its small, rounded leaves and ability to form a dense, carpet-like mat.
